methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Karman Industries is a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by David Tearse and Chiranjeev Kalra that has raised $11.5M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 private frontier-tech companies in Climate & Energy alongside 1 public comparable (LNZA), with particular reference to near-stage peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis used the $11.5M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applied a 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier, yielding $29M. Peer-median analysis examined three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A)—producing a median implied valuation of $150M, with range of $110M to $200M. The disparity between methods reflects typical stage and maturity differences within the peer set.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $24M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $89M across the two methods. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the peer median given Karman's earlier stage relative to Series A comparables, and the implicit assumption that total raised serves as a reasonable proxy for current post-seed valuation. Additional clarity on recent financing terms and company-specific traction metrics would refine this range.
